div.head: The Presidents Special Counsel (Clifford) to the Acting Secretary of State (Lovett)
div.p: Black 93. I have your White 174. Supra. Have discussed the matter with the President and he directs me to inform you that your existing instructions are cancelled.The expression “existing instructions” possibly refers to Presidential authorization for Mr. Lovett to use the statement. If you are asked questions on WednesdayOctober 13, the date of Mr. Lovett’s press conference. you are to make no comment.

We note with great interest the plan of the Secretary to discuss alternative language on arrival in Paris. We feel it would be most helpful if he would include Ben CohenBenjamin V. Cohen, Alternative Representative in the United States Delegation at the first part of the Third Session of the General Assembly at Paris. in such discussion because of the latter’s familiarity with our problems here.Acting Secretary Lovett quoted this paragraph in part to Secretary Marshall in telegram Telmar 63, October 12, 1 p. m., to Paris (501.BB Palestine/10–1248).
Will appreciate your forwarding to us as soon as possible any new language. If you prefer we can always be reached by telephone.
